Dreamsinger is one of the Five Blades of Corusk. It has a blade of white steel and is set with moonstones. When you are not attuned to it, you gain a +1 bonus to your attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
• When you are attuned to it, you gain the following benefits.
• You gain a +3 bonus to your attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
• You have a +2 bonus to your AC and to any saving throw.
• You can sense automatically the exact position of another Blade of Corusk as long as it is within 1 mile of you.
Vex (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage to the creature, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.
Finesse: When making an attack with a Finesse weapon, use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Proficiency: martial, rapier Source: Treasures from Oerth p. 34 (Homebrew)

item.name: Equalizer of Gran March [5.5e]
item.detail: longsword, legendary (requires attunement by a creature of neutral alignment)
item.text:
This silver longsword was created to be a tool for the balancing powers of the Multiverse. While you hold this magic weapon, you have a +3 bonus to your attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. When you successfully hit a creature of a non-neutral alignment or that's not unaligned, you can use a Bonus Action to invoke the balancing powers. The target must succeed on DC 19 Charisma saving throw or it is completely disintegrated. Once you use this property, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.
Sap (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.
Versatile: A Versatile weapon can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property. The weapon deals that damage when used with two hands to make a melee attack.
Proficiency: simple, longsword Source: Treasures from Oerth p. 34 (Homebrew)

item.name: Fire Wand of the Sueloise [5.5e]
item.text:
This magic wand may have been the device that caused the destruction of the Suel Empire. While you hold this magic wand, you can use a Magic Action to spend 3 charges and create a 60-foot Cube to a point that you can see within 120 feet of you. The Cube contains a rain of magical fire that burns everyone and everything. Each creature within the Cube must make a DC 19 Dexterity saving throw, taking 12d8 Fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. On each of your next turns, you can spend a Bonus Action to maintain the Cube, and each creature that starts its turn there or enters the Cube for the first time on a turn must make a DC 19 Dexterity saving throw, taking 12d8 Fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Objects not carried or worn within the cube other than those made of mithral and adamantine take 12d8 Fire damage at the end of each of your turns as long as the Cube lasts. When you spend the last charge, roll a d20. On a 10 or lower, the wand explodes in a Sphere of 60-foot radius centered on you. Each creature within the sphere must make a DC 21 Dexterity saving throw, taking 24d8 fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.
Recharge. The wand has 6 charges and recharges 1d6 spent charges daily at dawn.
Proficiency: simple, wand Source: Treasures from Oerth p. 34 (Homebrew)

item.name: Greenswathe [5.5e]
item.detail: scimitar, legendary (requires attunement by a druid)
item.text:
Greenswathe is one of the Five Blades of Corusk. It is made of a gold-colored alloy as hard as adamantine. When you are not attuned to it, you gain a +1 bonus to your attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. When you are attuned to it, you gain a +3 bonus to your attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. Additionally, when you successfully hit a target with this magic weapon, as a Bonus Action you deal 2d6 additional damage. Once you use this property three times, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.
Nick (Mastery): When you make the extra attack of the Light property, you can make it as part of the Attack action in· stead of as a Bonus Action. You can make this extra attack only once per turn.
Finesse: When making an attack with a Finesse weapon, use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Light: When you take the Attack action on your turn and. attack with a Light weapon, you can make one extra attack as a Bonus Action later on the same turn. That extra attack must be made with a different Light weapon, and you don't add your ability modifier to the extra attack's damage unless that modifier is negative. For example, you can attack with a Shortsword in one hand and a Dagger in the other using the Attack action and a Bonus Action, but you don't add your Strength or Dexterity modifier to the damage roll of the Bonus Action unless that modifier is negative.
Proficiency: martial, scimitar Source: Treasures from Oerth p. 36 (Homebrew), Player's Handbook (2024) p. 215

item.name: Harmonizer [5.5e]
item.detail: shortsword, legendary (requires attunement by a rogue)
item.text:
Harmonizer is one of the Five Blades of Corusk. Across the shiny surface of this magic weapon are etched images of a wizard performing a ritual over five swords. You gain a +3 bonus to your attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. When you attune to this magic item, the sword creates a clone of you and each creature Allied to you within 120 feet of you. The clones are Fey, appear in an unoccupied space within 30 feet of you and they are Hostile to you and your allies.
Vex (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage to the creature, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.
Finesse: When making an attack with a Finesse weapon, use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Light: When you take the Attack action on your turn and. attack with a Light weapon, you can make one extra attack as a Bonus Action later on the same turn. That extra attack must be made with a different Light weapon, and you don't add your ability modifier to the extra attack's damage unless that modifier is negative. For example, you can attack with a Shortsword in one hand and a Dagger in the other using the Attack action and a Bonus Action, but you don't add your Strength or Dexterity modifier to the damage roll of the Bonus Action unless that modifier is negative.
Proficiency: martial, shortsword Source: Treasures from Oerth p. 36 (Homebrew), Player's Handbook (2024) p. 215

item.name: Horn of the Azure Sea [5.5e]
item.detail: legendary (requires attunement by a creature of evil alignment)
item.text:
This magic horn was originally created by an evil cleric. This magic item functions only at night on the open sea. As a Magic Action, you can blow the horn and a Huge ghost ship appears in an unoccupied space on the sea within 360 feet of you. Each creature other than you and your allies that can see that ghost ship must succeed on a DC 19 Wisdom saving throw or has the Frightened condition for 1 minute. The ghost ship disappears after 1 minute or earlier if you dismiss it as a Bonus Action. When the ship disappears, the condition ends on the affected creatures. Once you use this property, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.
Source: Treasures from Oerth p. 37 (Homebrew), Player's Handbook (2024) p. 215

item.name: Iron Crown of the Bandit Kingdom [5.5e]
item.text:
This metal headgear was fashioned for fighters. When you wear this magic crown, you have Immunity to the Charmed and Frightened condition.
Source: Treasures from Oerth p. 37 (Homebrew), Player's Handbook (2024) p. 215

item.name: Mace of St. Cuthbert [5.5e]
item.detail: mace, legendary (requires attunement by a cleric or paladin of a lawful good alignment)
item.text:
This magic mace is known to be a relic created by St.Cuthbert himself. You gain a +3 bonus to your attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. When you hit a Fiend or an Undead with this magic weapon, that creature takes an extra 2d6 radiant damage. If the target has 25 hit points or fewer, it must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or be destroyed. On a successful save, that creature has the Frightened condition until the end of your next turn. While you hold this weapon, it sheds Bright Light in a 20-foot radius, and Dim Light for an additional 20 feet. As a Magic Action, you can spend one or more charges to cast one of the following spells:
• bless (1 charge) • remove curse (2 charge) • heal (3 charges) • resurrection (5 charges)
When you spend the last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, your Intelligence score lowers by 1. If your Intelligence score reaches 3 in such a manner, you become an NPC Celestial servant of St.Cuthbert.
Recharge. This magic mace has 5 charges and recharges 1d4+1 spent charges daily at dawn
Sap (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.
Proficiency: simple, mace Source: Treasures from Oerth p. 37 (Homebrew), Player's Handbook (2024) p. 215

item.name: Prism of Greyhawk [5.5e]
item.detail: legendary (requires attunement by a bard, sorcerer, or wizard)
item.text:
This crystal tool was originally enchanted to enhance the prism's ability to create dazzling colors. As a Magic Action, you can cast the prismatic ray or the prismatic wall spells(Spell Save DC 19). Once you cast one of these two spells, you can't use this property again until you finish a Long Rest. This magic item functions only if you are in sunlight.
Source: Treasures from Oerth p. 37 (Homebrew), Player's Handbook (2024) p. 215

item.name: Red Armor of the Hellfurnaces [5.5e]
item.text:
This scale armor was cut from the back plates of adult or ancient red dragons. Many warriors from the Yeomanry died in battle to kill those creatures, and the best armorers were hired to rework the plates into a wearable armor. While wearing this magic scale armor you gain the following benefits.
• You gain a +3 bonus to your AC. • You gain Resistance to Fire damage.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Proficiency: medium armor Source: Treasures from Oerth p. 38 (Homebrew), Player's Handbook (2024) p. 219

item.name: Scepter of the Forgotten City [5.5e]
item.detail: scale mail, legendary (requires attunement by a cleric or wizard)
item.text:
A relic of a lost civilization, this wandlike scepter is a 2-foot-long shaft of silver with a large red gem at the tip. It was used by some of the original lords of the Suel Empire; when that kingdom was turned into the Sea of Dust, the wand was lost. A recent expedition from the Yeomanry rediscovered the wand, and those explorers claimed that it came from the Forgotten City itself. Whether or not that claim is true, the scepter certainly contains some of the greatness that built the lost empire, and much of the madness that destroyed it. As a Magic Action, you can spend one charge to raise your Intelligence and Wisdom scores to 25 for the next 24 hours. Each time you spend one charge, you must make a DC 20 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, your Constitution score lowers by 1. When your Constitution reaches 0, you die and your corpse raises as a wraith in the next 24 hours. When you spend the last charge, the scepter disappears.
Recharge. The Scepter has 25 charges and cannot be recharged.
Proficiency: simple, rod Source: Treasures from Oerth p. 38 (Homebrew)

item.name: Shield of Greyhawk [5.5e]
item.text:
This magic shield bears Greyhawk's coat of arms and was originally created by the Society of the Magi. It now hangs in the audience hall of the Lord Mayor's mansion. While you hold this magic shield, you gain the following benefits.
• You gain a +3 bonus to your AC.
• You can cast the dispel evil and good spell. Once you use this property twice, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.
Utilize Action to Don or Doff.
Proficiency: shield Source: Treasures from Oerth p. 38 (Homebrew), Player's Handbook (2024) p. 219

item.name: Silver Crown of Veluna [5.5e]
item.detail: shield, legendary (requires attunement by a cleric of good alignment)
item.text:
Encrusted with small green gems, this silver crown recently appeared among the ruling clerics of Veluna. The Canon himself has often worn it, though he may sometimes loan it to trusted agents of Veluna when they embark on important, holy missions. While you wear this magic item, you gain the following benefits.
• Your Wisdom score increases by 2 up to a maximum of 22. • You are under the permanent effect of a protection from good and evil spell, but you are protected only by Aberrations, Elementals, Fiends, and Undead.
Proficiency: shield Source: Treasures from Oerth p. 40 (Homebrew)

item.name: Soul Gem [5.5e]
item.text:
This glowing magic gem floats in the air, slowly rotating around you. As a Magic Action, you can spend 1 charge and the gem makes a blast of negative energy towards a creature that you can see within 60 feet. The target must make a DC 21 Constitution saving throw, taking 10d10 necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. If the target reaches 0 hp, it immediately dies, its soul is entrapped within the gem, and it can't be resurrected, unless the gem is broken. This magic gem has AC 20, 50 hit points, and has Immunity to Poison, Necrotic, Psychic damage, and to Bludgeoning, Piercing and Slashing damage made with non magical attacks. Each time you spend a charge, you consume a soul trapped within the gem. The DM chooses which soul is consumed. If its soul is consumed, that creature can be resurrected only by a wish spell.
Recharge. This magic gem recharges 1 charge each time a soul is trapped within.
Proficiency: shield Source: Treasures from Oerth p. 40 (Homebrew)

item.name: Stalker [5.5e]
item.detail: longsword, legendary (requires attunement by a ranger)
item.text:
Stalker is a one of the Five Blades of Corusk. It is a longsword with a blue steel blade and a hilt, pommel, and crosspiece of black adamantine. Wisps of shadowstuff seem to move silently across its surface. When you are not attuned to it, you gain a +1 bonus to your attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. When you are attuned to it, you gain a +3 bonus to your attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, and you gain advantage on any Dexterity (Stealth) and Wisdom (Survival) check.
Sap (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.
Versatile: A Versatile weapon can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property. The weapon deals that damage when used with two hands to make a melee attack.
Proficiency: martial, longsword Source: Treasures from Oerth p. 41 (Homebrew), Player's Handbook (2024) p. 215

item.name: Sword of Azor'alq [5.5e]
item.text:
You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. It has the following additional properties.
Crush Evil: When you successfully hit an Aberration or a Fiend, the target must succeed on DC 19 Constitution saving throw or it is disintegrated. Once you successfully use this property, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.
Detect Evil: As a Magic Action, you can exactly locate any Aberration or Fiend within 1 mile of you. When you use this property three times, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.
Spells: As a Magic Action, you can cast the fly spell. Once you cast this spell, you can't cast it again until you finish a Long Rest.
Sentience: The Sword of Azor'alq is a sentient neutral good weapon with an Intelligence of 17, a Wisdom of 10, and a Charisma of 16. It has hearing and Darkvision out of a range of 120 feet. The weapon can speak, read, and understand Common and Elvish, and can communicate with its wielder telepathically. Its voice is proud and noble. While you are attuned to it, the weapon also understands every language you know.
Personality: The Sword of Azor'alq is very proud and does not wish to serve anyone who is not an important baron or king. It also expect its owner to provide it with a new scabbard, with gems fixed to it. In general, the sword thinks it is in charge of any owner. The sword especially hates Fiends and Aberrations and actively seeks out to destroy them.
Sap (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.
Versatile: A Versatile weapon can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property. The weapon deals that damage when used with two hands to make a melee attack.
Proficiency: martial, longsword Source: Treasures from Oerth p. 41 (Homebrew), Player's Handbook (2024) p. 215

item.name: Talisman of Zagy [5.5e]
item.text:
This magic item is an oddly-shaped bit of extrusive rock, shaped and roughly polished. When you attune to this magic item, roll a d20. On a 10 or lower, this magic item gives you a Curse. As long as you have that Curse, your Speed is halved and you have Disadvantage on any Strength Test. The Curse can be removed by a greater restoration spell or greater magic. On a 11 or higher, this magic item grants you the ability to cast the wish spell as a Magic Action. Once you cast the spell, the talisman disappears.
Proficiency: martial, longsword Source: Treasures from Oerth p. 41 (Homebrew), Player's Handbook (2024) p. 215

item.name: The Edge [5.5e]
item.detail: bastard sword, legendary (requires attunement by a fighter)
item.text:
The Edge is one of the Five Blades of Corusk. This magic weapon is a bastard sword whose blade is made of graysh steel. The hilt of the sword is covered in soft suede leather. Jutting from the pommel, three blackened daggers point outward like venomous fango. You gain a +3 bonus to your attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. As a Magic Action, you can order the three blackened daggers to fly and hit a creature that you can see within 30 feet of you. You can direct the daggers to one or more targets. For each time that is hit by a dagger, the target must make on a DC 19 Constitution saving throw, taking 4d10 Poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Once you use this property, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.
Sap (Mastery): If you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.
Versatile: A Versatile weapon can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property. The weapon deals that damage when used with two hands to make a melee attack.
Proficiency: martial, bastard sword Source: Treasures from Oerth p. 41 (Homebrew), Player's Handbook (2024) p. 215

item.name: Weeping Hexagram [5.5e]
item.detail: legendary (requires attunement by a clericsorcerer this magic item is a black iron ring inlaid with a
item.text:
bowed hexagram. It seeps blood when exposed to sunlight. It was created by clerics of Tharizdun. While you wear this magic ring, you can spend one or more charges to cast one of the following spells (Spell Save DC 19):
• animate dead (1 charge) • darkness (1 charge) • Evard's black tentacles (2 charges) • creation (3 charges) • gate (5 charges)
When you spend the last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the ring disappears.
Recharge. The ring has 6 charges and recharges 1d6+1 spent charges daily at dawn.
Source: Treasures from Oerth p. 42 (Homebrew), Player's Handbook (2024) p. 215

item.name: White Armor of the Griff Mountains [5.5e]
item.text:
This scale armor is cut from the hide of adult or ancient white dragons and crafted into armor by master armorers. While you wear this magic scale armor, you gain the following effects:
• You gain a +3 bonus to your AC. • You gain resistance to cold damage.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Proficiency: medium armor Source: Treasures from Oerth p. 42 (Homebrew), Player's Handbook (2024) p. 219
